purpose: Cadence interference material for Bub YouTube Writer's three writer models (Claude Sonnet, Minimax M2.5, Grok Fast). Injected into system prompts of generate-script, suggest-hooks, and editors-table routes to disrupt RLHF-induced rhythm homogenization.

transcripts.notes: Strongest fragmentation in the pool. 'No. Hell no. Of course not.' triad patterns. Use when you want maximum permission to break into fragments.
Widest sentence-length range in pool. Opens with character dialogue (Jamie/Locke) which demonstrates abrupt register shifts.
Opens with Robert Greene quote, then pivots hard into Jason cadence. Good demonstration of register switching.
Strong instructional cadence that still maintains rhythmic variety. Good model for tutorial-type YouTube content.
Most conversational of the pool. 'Right? Right.' patterns. Mid-sentence audience addresses.
Heavy use of one-line constructions ('Indeed. Thoughts are things.'). Good for affirmative rhythm.
More narrative-heavy with story elements. Use when rhythmic variety over longer prose stretches is needed.
Instructional content with rhythmic breaks. 'Deflate them, you must.' syntactic inversions. Shortest transcript.

selection_guidance.cost_optimization: If cost is a concern, prefer shorter transcripts (05, 06, 07, 08) to reduce per-generation tokens.
selection_guidance.signal_maximization: If rhythm signal is weak in output, prefer higher-rated transcripts (01, 02, 03).
selection_guidance.variety_rotation: Random without replacement across the pool means each generation pulls a different combination — this is the core of the camouflage strategy.

dropped_candidates_needing_cleanup: 02_Loosh_is_emotional_energy_and_archons_feed_on_loosh.txt — contains Bob Monroe quote that confuses cadence signal; could be added if block quotes are stripped
In_the_beginning_God_created_the_heavens_the_Earth_and_energy_vampirism.txt — ~20 lines of Monroe block quote at the start; dominates cadence; would need heavier editing
future_expansion.additions_welcome: Any new transcript with high sentence-length variety, fragment use, and low AI-tell vocabulary density. Target ~1500-2500 words per transcript.
